title: Paracostus paradoxus
taxonomy: Paracostus paradoxus is classified as a species of plant in the family Costaceae, which is a group of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. The genus Paracostus is part of the family Costaceae and is closely related to the genus Costus. Paracostus paradoxus is a member of the order Zingiberales, which includes other plants such as ginger and bananas. This classification suggests a close evolutionary relationship with other plants in the Costaceae family. Paracostus paradoxus is also a type of rhizomatous perennial, which is a characteristic shared with many other members of the Costaceae family.

constituents: Paracostus paradoxus contains active constituents such as iridoids and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
